claim: Agentic eagerness is your ability to control how much decision-making GPT-5 makes vs. how much direction you provide, spanning from fully autonomous to single-task execution
actionable: Use the reasoning_effort parameter and system prompts to control GPT-5's decision-making autonomy—lower effort for fast targeted responses, higher for thorough exploration
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5

claim: Lower reasoning_effort setting reduces exploration depth and tool usage while improving efficiency and latency, cutting tokens and costs
actionable: Set reasoning_effort to low for faster responses with fewer tool calls when speed and cost matter more than comprehensiveness
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5

claim: Define clear context-gathering criteria in agent prompts: specify search depth, parallelization strategy, stop criteria (e.g., 70% convergence), and escalation rules
actionable: Create explicit guidance in agent system prompts for how to search, when to parallelize, and when to stop gathering context
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5

claim: Agents should have an escape hatch in context-gathering prompts allowing them to stop searching early if confident enough, even if not fully correct
actionable: Include permission in agent prompts to end context gathering early with best-effort answers rather than requiring exhaustive search
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5

claim: Tool preambles are structured progress updates GPT-5 provides while using tools, narrating its search status, step completion, and upfront plans for transparency
actionable: Configure tool preambles in your prompts to get real-time visibility into agent actions instead of batch results at the end
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5

claim: The responses API shows 78.2 vs 73.9 TAB Bench score improvement over chat completions because it reuses context across calls, improving agentic flows and reducing tokens
actionable: Use GPT-5's responses API instead of chat completions for agentic workflows to improve performance and reduce token costs through context reuse
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5

claim: GPT-5 excels at front-end development with frameworks like Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, and fonts like Inter and Manrope
actionable: When using GPT-5 for frontend development, prioritize these popular frameworks and libraries for best results
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5

claim: Tell GPT-5 to create its own excellence rubric (5-7 categories) for self-reflection when one-shotting web applications, improving output quality through self-evaluation
actionable: When asking GPT-5 to build complete applications in one shot, instruct it to create quality rubrics and iterate against them internally
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5

claim: Define codebase patterns by providing explicit rules on modularity, reusability, consistency, directory structure, and UIUX best practices in system prompts
actionable: Create detailed style guides and architecture documentation in system prompts when asking GPT-5 to work with existing codebases
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5

claim: GPT-5 is by default overly thorough in context gathering; Cursor team had to tone down maximize instructions and soften language around thoroughness
actionable: When using GPT-5 agents, avoid heavy maximize/exhaustive language that worked for previous models—dial back thoroughness instructions
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5

claim: Set verbosity API parameter to low to keep agent text outputs brief while using reasoning_effort to control internal thinking length separately
actionable: Control verbosity and reasoning_effort independently—reduce verbosity for cleaner outputs while keeping reasoning effort matched to task complexity
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5

claim: GPT-5 follows prompt instructions with surgical precision; conflicts or undefined edge cases can cause failures, making iterative prompt refinement essential
actionable: When writing prompts for GPT-5, ensure logical consistency and handle edge cases explicitly—use AI to help identify contradictions in your prompts
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5

claim: Minimal reasoning is the fastest reasoning mode for latency-sensitive use cases, but performance varies more with prompts, requiring clearer guidance and planning
actionable: Use minimal reasoning for speed-critical scenarios, but compensate with more explicit planning instructions and clear output formatting requests
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5

claim: Use Zapier agents to give GPT-5 access to 7000+ integrated tools via MCP, enabling agents to perform complex multi-service workflows
actionable: Connect Zapier as an MCP for your GPT-5 agents to access hundreds of external services and integrations
source_title: How to Make Better Prompts for GPT-5
